title: Security vulnerability in svg-sprite due to outdated svgo dependency
author: koraevad
body:
We're currently using svg-sprite plugin (version 2.0.4) in our project, and our security team has identified a vulnerability that affects our application.

The issue stems from an outdated dependency on svgo (https://github.com/svg/svgo). The current version of svg-sprite (2.0.4) uses svgo version 2.8.0, which contains a known security vulnerability:

CVE-2026-29074 (https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-29074)

Security advisory: https://github.com/svg/svgo/security/advisories/GHSA-xpqw-6gx7-v673

This vulnerability has been fixed in later versions of svgo. Could you please update the svgo dependency to the latest secure version in svg-sprite?

Thank you for your attention to this security issue.

If that would be possible

I checked https://www.npmjs.com/package/svg-sprite and https://github.com/svg-sprite/svg-sprite/releases

I found this by trying to track down where I get 

> npm warn deprecated inflight@1.0.6: This module is not supported, and leaks memory. Do not use it. Check out lru-cache if you want a good and tested way to coalesce async requests by a key value, which is much more comprehensive and powerful.

on `npm clean-install` of https://github.com/openstreetmap/iD

(though release would not fix it anyway, see https://github.com/svg-sprite/svg-sprite/issues/889 )

title: Refactoring svg-sprite  - finally
author: vegetable-bits
body:
I love this tool and have been using it for so many years. But we all know it's staging to aging.

After the original developer stepped down, the development slowed down. A lot of configurations don't work well anymore. I am interested in contributing, and here's my idea.

1. Remove unneeded options/functionality/complexity. We have 5 output modes; does really anyone use CSS sprites in 2025? Let's remove it and only keep `<view>`, `<symbol>`, and `<defs>`. Once done, release a new major version without new functionality.
2. Once 1.) is ready, let's modernize the code by upgrading all third-party libraries, then maybe split CLI and lib into 2 projects.

I am ready to contribute if there's interest from maintainers, but I am not sure I want to create another fork.

title: `SVGSpriter.add: "..." is not the local part of "..."` error on Windows
author: jefferyto
body:
When source SVGs are in subdirectories I get this error on Windows 11 (PowerShell in regular Windows, not WSL):

```
PS C:\Users\Jeff\Downloads\svg-sprite-test> npm run compile:sprite

> svg-sprite-test@0.1.0 compile:sprite
> svg-sprite --log info --config sprite.config.json "images/./**/*.svg"

2025-08-11 14:08:05.050 - info: Using 4 threads
2025-08-11 14:08:05.055 - info: Created spriter instance
2025-08-11 14:08:05.110 - error: SVGSpriter.add: "icons/menu.svg" is not the local part of "C:\Users\Jeff\Downloads\svg-sprite-test\images\icons\menu.svg" SVGSpriter.add: "icons/menu.svg" is not the local part of "C:\Users\Jeff\Downloads\svg-sprite-test\images\icons\menu.svg"
C:\Users\Jeff\Downloads\svg-sprite-test\node_modules\svg-sprite\lib\svg-sprite.js:125
            throw e;
            ^

ArgumentError: SVGSpriter.add: "icons/menu.svg" is not the local part of "C:\Users\Jeff\Downloads\svg-sprite-test\images\icons\menu.svg"
    at SVGSpriter.add (C:\Users\Jeff\Downloads\svg-sprite-test\node_modules\svg-sprite\lib\svg-sprite.js:123:23)
    at C:\Users\Jeff\Downloads\svg-sprite-test\node_modules\svg-sprite\bin\svg-sprite.js:279:17
    at Array.forEach (<anonymous>)
    at Object.<anonymous> (C:\Users\Jeff\Downloads\svg-sprite-test\node_modules\svg-sprite\bin\svg-sprite.js:267:6)
    at Module._compile (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1688:14)
    at Object..js (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1820:10)
    at Module.load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1423:32)
    at Function._load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1246:12)
    at TracingChannel.traceSync (node:diagnostics_channel:322:14)
    at wrapModuleLoad (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:235:24)

Node.js v22.18.0
PS C:\Users\Jeff\Downloads\svg-sprite-test>
```

package.json:

```json
{
  "name": "svg-sprite-test",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "license": "UNLICENSED",
  "private": true,
  "devDependencies": {
    "svg-sprite": "^2.0.4"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "compile:sprite": "svg-sprite --log info --config sprite.config.json \"images/./**/*.svg\""
  }
}
```

sprite.config.json:

```json
{
  "mode": {
    "view": {
      "dest": ".",
      "sprite": "sprite.svg",
      "bust": false
    }
  }
}
```

title: Glob - Inflight - Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime
author: Luen
body:
Security vulnerability identified by Snyk within the `svg-sprite` package's dependencies. 
The issue is tied to the `inflight` package, which is a transitive dependency through `glob`. According to Snyk, the vulnerability is registered as [SNYK-JS-INFLIGHT-6095116](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JS-INFLIGHT-6095116) and [CWE-772: Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/772.html).

### Issue Description
The `inflight` package, which is used to prevent parallel execution of async tasks with the same key, has been reported to have a security vulnerability. 

### Relevant Context
`glob` version 9 and onwards have moved away from using callbacks to promises, leading to the removal of `inflight` from its dependencies due to the API changes. This evolution is discussed in the following `node-glob` GitHub issues:
- [isaacs/node-glob#435](https://github.com/isaacs/node-glob/issues/435)
- [isaacs/node-glob#573](https://github.com/isaacs/node-glob/issues/573)

Additionally, the `inflight` package itself has it's own issues:
- [isaacs/inflight#5](https://github.com/isaacs/inflight/issues/5)
- [isaacs/inflight#19](https://github.com/isaacs/inflight/issues/19)

### Suggested Action
Given that `glob` has evolved past the need for `inflight`, it may be beneficial for `svg-sprite` to update its dependencies accordingly to a version of `glob` that does not rely on `inflight` V9/V10.

title: [v2.0.2] - Can we upgrade v2.0.2 to have lodash version that fixes the lodash.trim vulnerability
author: classicmike
body: Hi all. I would like to introduce a change to bump up the 2.0.2 release of svg-sprite to fix the following vulnerability https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-29mw-wpgm-hmr9 Here is a screenshot of the vulnerability after NPM audit: My fix will involve replacing the individual lodash packages with 4.,17.21 version of the entire lodash package. It's currently on my own fork here: https://github.com/classicmike/svg-sprite/commit/f31c8003297b313aa3d2db29cc1cbb8f6be86f3c Questions on this if we were to go ahead with this fix, where can I redirect my PR to? I'm asking this because it seems like this repo is working towards a version 3 which already has breaking changes. My assumption would be is to create a new branch on this repo based on the v2.0.2 tag then I PR to that new branch and publish a new v2.0.3 release from there. Anybody managing this project could you please let me know. Thanks.
